best_edges.action: Protect and use as baseline PnL engine; avoid broad structure pruning.
best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback/reclaim continuation with moderate VWAP distance.
best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the top profile: 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, 12.0 minute median hold, no slow >=12h trades. Worst MAE is -403.2, so it is productive but not tail-free.

best_edges.action: Protect; only expand through isolated split tests or small admission probes.
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but contributes $985.6, 197.1 average PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, and no slow trades. Prior charm-wall admission probe improved score by +217 and net profit by +$221.

best_edges.action: Protect from direct tightening; research companion split instead.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte contributes $1305 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with no slow trades. Prior direct 1DTE quality tightening hurt score and net profit, so the edge is real but sensitive.

best_edges.action: Protect clean winners; test only state splits around delayed progress and high-extension tails.
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1237.7 over 34 trades with 5.3 minute median hold and average MAE -74.3, but owns the global -527 MAE. Extended-VWAP clean winners show $674.9 PnL, median MAE -14.9, and 1.7 minute median hold, so blunt extension blocking is dangerous.

best_edges.action: Keep available; expand only with hold-time and MAE guardrails.
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long contributes $721 over 10 trades, no slow trades, worst MAE -208.25, and median MFE 77.8. It is not the largest engine, but its tail profile is cleaner than pullback/charm-pin.

broken_edges.action: Do not use broad global entry gates based on these fields; prefer profile-aware splits or surgical veto tuning.
broken_edges.evidence: Hidden signatures such as es_structure_pullback_from_high <= 3.25 can reduce worst MAE to -403.2 but retain only 72.6% PnL. es_structure_rising_vwap and 1DTE exclusion retain only about 79-82% PnL and do not remove the -527 tail.
broken_edges.problem: Single-threshold MAE filters are too expensive and often fail to remove the worst tail.

broken_edges.action: Freeze direct quality tightening; research a separate 1DTE pullback companion instead.
broken_edges.evidence: Prior 1DTE local quality gate reduced score by -594, net profit by -$558.5, trades by 7, and worsened worst MAE from -527 to -603.75.
broken_edges.problem: Direct breakout_long_1dte tightening damages the edge.

broken_edges.action: Do not re-test broad pullback TP/VWAP expansion from the current base.
broken_edges.evidence: Prior pullback expansion increased net profit slightly but worsened drawdown to 2%, worst MAE to -2026.5, added a slow trade, and cut objective score by -1731.
broken_edges.problem: Wider pullback admission/targets create unacceptable latent heat in a no-stop system.

broken_edges.action: Treat stale fallback as under-tested and low priority for expansion.
broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE contributes only $162.8 across 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale subset has 2 trades, $71 PnL, 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, 52.6 minute median hold, and -250.25 worst MAE.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE stale fallback is inefficient relative to core VALID_0DTE edges.

broken_edges.action: Keep dormant unless explicitly activated as bounded discovery tests.
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long are listed but produced zero trades in the latest run.
broken_edges.problem: Several active router profiles are dead or unreachable under current routing/config.

market_regime_read: Current evidence is a short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only regime with low realized drawdown but meaningful hidden MAE. There are 107 completed trades, 100% win rate, no realized losses, and no slow >=12h trades, but average MAE is about -101.7 and worst MAE is -527. The portfolio edge is concentrated in VALID_0DTE pullback/charm profiles plus a profitable 1DTE fallback. Since TP-before-MAE is only 0.9% and MFE-before-MAE is only 9.3%, the risk-adjusted bottleneck is not win rate; it is separating fast recoverable heat from slow/delayed adverse paths without pruning the profitable fast-winner population.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Worst charm-pin tail has no early positive progress, 80.3 minutes to first +1.5 points, high VWAP extension, negative acceleration, and large pullback/rolling-high distance, while protected extended-VWAP winners resolve quickly with median hold 1.7 minutes and median MAE -14.9.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A static config proxy blocks clean extended-VWAP winners and sacrifices more PnL than the MAE reduction is worth.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: first positive movement, first +1.5/+2.0 point time, MFE before MAE, first-15m MFE/MAE, plus profile and VWAP-extension context.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high ES-VWAP extension or recent-high rejection that fail to make early positive progress after entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and no slow >=12h trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: VALID_1DTE_ONLY is profitable but has relatively high average MAE; previous direct 1DTE tightening hurt, implying the opportunity is a split, not a stricter version of breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router ordering may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Option data state, 1DTE map validity, ES pullback/reclaim state, structure score, VWAP distance/slope, and resulting profile attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of leader, and total score does not degrade.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pin-avoid concepts may isolate high charm-wall/pin-risk states currently mixed into charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Activation may be unreachable or may steal clean charm-pin/charm-wall winners instead of isolating tails.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin Avoid Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, charm wall/pin proximity, profile PnL, protected fast-winner retention, and combined charm profile MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk state currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner count is not materially reduced.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Pullback_long is the largest PnL engine but still carries -403.2 worst MAE and average MAE -110.2.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Config-only weak-structure filters block profitable pullbacks because many weak-looking pullbacks recover quickly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Pullback profile attribution, early path progress, ES structure distances, VWAP acceleration, reclaim distance, and hold-time outcome.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entries with weak structure, poor early MFE, or delayed recovery behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and keeping total PnL/exposure hour flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Call-wall acceptance has a tiny but clean sample with 1 trade, $91 PnL, 455 PnL/exposure hour, and -11.7 MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: One-trade evidence is too weak; loosening admission can admit low-quality wall-chase entries.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Holdout Expansion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Call wall distance, call-volume acceleration, net DEX, forward DEX ratio, profile PnL/exposure, and MAE by accepted wall state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance state with strong call-volume increase and clean ES structure.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 additional trades with positive standalone PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=250, worst MAE <= -150, and total net profit >=98% of leader.

next_experiments.config_bias: Activate exactly one dormant valid-0DTE pin-avoid profile through the valid-0DTE router list and keep tuning minimal. Treat this as an attribution/split experiment, not a replacement for charm_pin_pullback_long.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether pin-avoid routing can separate charm-pin tails from clean charm-pin/charm-wall winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The -527 charm-pin tail and several threshold-miss deep-MAE rows are a distinct pin/extension avoidance state that should be routed separately rather than globally vetoed.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Router Split Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined charm-pin/charm-wall/pin-avoid PnL, worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, and total risk-adjusted score.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample and router cannibalization risk are high; reject if total PnL falls below 98% of leader or protected fast winners are reduced materially.

next_experiments.config_bias: Add or activate a companion 1DTE pullback concept if legal. Leave breakout_long_1dte thresholds stable because prior direct tightening failed.
next_experiments.goal: Split profitable 1DTE flow into breakout and pullback/reclaim regimes without suppressing current fallback PnL.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY has real PnL but elevated average MAE; a companion profile may capture pullback-like entries with lower heat while preserving breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average MAE, total net profit retention, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Router overlap can cannibalize the current profitable 20-trade breakout_long_1dte sample.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use only narrow boundary nudges around the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection family. Avoid broad ES-VWAP max-distance, setup-quality floors, or global structure gates.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce deep MAE in the known extended-VWAP threshold-miss family while preserving extended-VWAP fast winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The enabled extended-VWAP rejection veto nearly matches several deep-MAE rows; carefully widening one boundary at a time may reduce MAE with less PnL loss than broad filters.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Threshold-Miss Boundary Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total PnL >=98%, retained extended-VWAP protected winners >=90%, and score.
next_experiments.risk: Some threshold misses are profitable winners and one representative bad-row is also in fast_winners, so over-capture can damage the edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep pullback_long core admission conservative; test only split-style or local heat classification. Do not repeat broad VWAP-distance/TP expansion.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether pullback_long contains a separable deep-heat subregime that can be reduced without harming the main PnL engine.
next_experiments.hypothesis: pullback_long is the largest edge but its worst MAE and average MAE indicate a tail-heavy subset; a companion classifier or profile-aware gate may improve risk-adjusted quality.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Split Research
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long PnL retention, pullback worst MAE, total PnL/exposure hour, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Pullback weak-structure states are often profitable; broad filters can remove too much throughput.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run a bounded expansion/holdout only if it can be isolated to call_wall_acceptance_long. Do not loosen global call-volume or DEX gates across profiles.
next_experiments.goal: Test whether the single clean call-wall acceptance trade is an emerging edge or just noise.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Call-wall acceptance shows excellent one-trade efficiency and very low MAE, but sample size is too small; a bounded admission probe can test reachability.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Acceptance Sample Builder
next_experiments.primary_metric: Standalone call_wall_acceptance_long PnL/exposure, trade count, worst MAE, and total PnL retention.
next_experiments.risk: One-trade evidence is weak; reject quickly if new trades dilute PnL/exposure or introduce material MAE.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate STALE_0DTE/no-0DTE behavior only; avoid affecting VALID_0DTE or the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce low-efficiency stale fallback exposure without changing the profitable core routes.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE fallback has low PnL/exposure and longer holds, especially breakout_long_no_0dte; a stale-specific trim may improve risk-adjusted quality.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, total net profit retention >=98%, and unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 stale trades; overfitting may remove useful fallback insurance.

research_notes: The next useful work is regime discovery, not another broad pruning pass. The leader is already robust, and most single-threshold filters either retain too little PnL or fail to remove the worst MAE. Preserve the real engines: pullback_long, breakout_long_1dte, charm_pin_pullback_long fast winners, charm_wall_breakaway_long, and squeeze_long. For new-regime work, prioritize router/profile splits: dormant pin-avoid activation, 1DTE pullback companion, and profile-aware tail classifiers. Any config-only experiment should be judged by total score, >=98% net-profit retention, protected fast-winner retention, and no new slow >=12h exposure.
risk_controls: Reject experiments that reduce total net profit below 98% of leader unless worst MAE improves materially and repeatably.
Reject experiments that reduce completed trades by more than 10% without improving score and MAE.
Track protected fast_winners, low_mae_winners, high_pnl_winners, and extended_vwap_winners separately; do not accept a filter that blocks these populations without a stronger tail benefit.
Keep slow >=12h trades at zero; this strategy has no stop loss and latent hold-time risk matters more than realized loss count.
Do not re-run direct breakout_long_1dte tightening or broad pullback TP/VWAP expansion; both have negative memory.
For dormant activations, activate one concept at a time and require standalone attribution so cannibalization is visible.
Use the current leader run_029 as the benchmark, not the slightly weaker repeated run_031, because run_029 has higher score, higher net profit, lower worst MAE, and better PnL/exposure.
